steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims that some modern southwestern irrigation methods have a lineage predating Lake Sabrina (completed 1908). The supporting quotation must show a current method whose origin goes back well before 1908.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘"Sprinkler irrigation systems, which were developed in the late 20th century, are a convenient technique of irrigating that makes use of machinery in order to spray water in all directions. Many areas of the southwestern United States are currently irrigated by this method."’: originated after 1908 — does not establish a pre-1908 lineage. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘"In the decades following the Second World War, irrigation of arid areas for agriculture increased to such a degree that it now accounts for roughly 70% of the world's water usage."’: discusses growth of irrigation post-1945, no connection to pre-1908 origins. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘"The irrigation system developed by the Hohokam people in what is now central Arizona in the 7th century CE was simple but made use of hydraulic engineering design principles that are still utilized by today's engineers."’: links a modern method directly to a 7th-century origin — over 1,200 years before 1908. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘"The importance of the development of irrigation infrastructure in the American Southwest today cannot be overstated, since it is the most common means of conveying water for food production."’: speaks to current importance only, not historical lineage. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
